description: To bite: into the fruit, through the thread, the dog that doesn't. Teeth ('kulai') closing on something. Food-first, like all of Phi's edge-words.
sound_symbolism: 'ki' sharp, 'wa' the thing met, 'te' the teeth closing — the event in order.
grammatical_notes: Transitive: 'mia lurekoi kiwate.' (I bite the fruit.) Reassurance: 'nolika ma kiwate.' (The animal doesn't bite.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The mindful eater's first instruction is to notice the bite itself — the verb marks where attention meets appetite.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The first test of food, rope, and coin alike was the bite — the mouth as the body's original instrument of judgment.
